evidence.scientific_clarity: Architecture diagram clearly conveys the RetroDiff pipeline with labeled Retrieval and Generation group boxes, directional solid arrows for data flow, and dashed arrows for control/feedback — exactly matching the brief.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are set; pdffonts confirms Arial CID TrueType embedded (no Type 3 fonts); font sizes 7.5–9pt are legible; no DejaVu Sans fallback occurred.
evidence.palette: Categorical colors (blue/purple/green/amber) are distinct and not red-green; dark navy vs. gray for data/control arrows respects directional discipline without colorblind traps.
evidence.layout_density: Nodes span the full 10-unit width with sensible spacing; legend fits in lower-left without overlap; no gaping whitespace; y-range (0–5) is well utilized.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly identifies both modules, explains solid vs. dashed arrow semantics, and notes derivation from data.json; legend placement (lower-left) is clear and non-overlapping.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts reports GOFYPY+Arial-BoldMT and GOFYPY+ArialMT as CID TrueType Identity-H embedded fonts; no Type 3 fonts present; script also performs a runtime Type-3 check.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (32KB), figure.png (115KB), figure.svg (11KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the system, describes both modules and their roles, explains arrow conventions (solid=data flow, dashed=control/feedback), and notes the diagram source; appropriate for an architecture figure with no numerical data.

overall_strengths: Perfect vector output with embedded Arial TrueType fonts (pdf.fonttype=42, no Type 3), with an automated runtime font-type verification step in the generation script.
Clean, well-structured architecture diagram with clear group boundaries, consistent color palette, and a self-explanatory legend — caption accurately mirrors all visual elements.
overall_weaknesses: data.json is missing from the deliverable directory (referenced in brief and script), so the figure cannot be regenerated without it.
Font sizes (7.5–9pt) are on the small side for a printed figure; a reviewer might request slightly larger text for legibility at reduced scale.
